channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: West Ham have fresh hope - but Spurs now in uncharted territory
item.description: West Ham's thumping win over Wolves not only boosts their own Premier League survival hopes, but plunges Tottenham into the drop zone - and to new depths.

item.title: Could Guardiola's best and Arteta's worst month decide title?
item.description: As the Premier League season approaches its finale, April could be the defining month for leaders Arsenal and closest challengers Manchester City. BBC Sport looks at the stats to explain why.

item.title: De Zerbi 'not surprised' by reaction to Greenwood comments
item.description: Tottenham manager Roberto de Zerbi says he was "not surprised" by the reaction from supporters over comments he made about Mason Greenwood.

item.title: A tough season, playing angry, and messaging Michael Owen
item.description: Ollie Watkins speaks to Kelly Somers about Aston Villa, England and his World Cup dream.

item.title: Fernandez sorry but will miss Man City - Rosenior
item.description: Chelsea midfielder Enzo Fernandez is still unavailable for the upcoming home match against Manchester City despite a recent apology, head coach Liam Rosenior says.

item.title: EFL preview: Open-top buses booked as finish line comes into view
item.description: Promotions within grasp, relegation six-pointers - here are five things to look out for in the English Football League this weekend.

item.title: Nike investigating issue with World Cup kits
item.description: Sportswear giant Nike is investigating a design issue with the shirts set to be worn by several teams - including England - at this summer's World Cup.

item.title: Guardiola would 'love' Silva to stay at Man City
item.description: Pep Guardiola says he would "love" captain Bernardo Silva to "finish his career" at Manchester City but it is up to the player to make the decision on whether he stays or leaves this summer.

item.title: Consultant leads review into Old Firm cup disorder
item.description: Sports event consultant Mark Blackbourne will lead the Scottish FA's investigation into the disorder that followed the Scottish Cup quarter-final between Rangers and Celtic in March.

item.title: Sutton's predictions v boxing promoter & Arsenal fan Frank Warren
item.description: Chris Sutton takes on boxing promoter Frank Warren plus the BBC readers and AI with his predictions for this weekend's Premier League fixtures.

item.title: Is Fernandes' own goal the worst of all time?
item.description: Porto's Martim Fernandes scored a bizarre own goal for Porto against Nottingham Forest on Thursday, but how does it compare to the worst of all-time?

item.title: Howe '100% committed' to Newcastle but 'I just need support'
item.description: Head coach Eddie Howe says he is "100% committed" to Newcastle United, but it is "very difficult" to look too far beyond the end of the season.

item.title: I feel complete backing from owners and fans - Slot
item.description: Liverpool manager Arne Slot insists he feels "complete support" from the club's hierarchy and fans despite a run of poor on-field performances and growing discontent about his future as manager

item.title: Spurs lead race for Robertson - Saturday's gossip
item.description: Tottenham in pole position to sign Andy Robertson, Manchester City in talks for Morten Hjulmand, Arsenal monitoring Anthony Gordon and Kees Smit attracting Premier League interest.

item.title: We asked fans of Premier League clubs how they are feeling - find out the results
item.description: As the Premier League returns after a three-week hiatus, we asked fans how they are feeling about their club.

item.title: Green Brigade return may boost title bid - O'Neill
item.description: Martin O'Neill thinks the return of the Green Brigade ultras group following the lifting of their suspension will be "a boost" to Celtic's title challenge.

item.title: West Ham thrash Wolves to move out of relegation zone
item.description: Taty Castellanos and Konstantinos Mavropanos both score twice as West Ham beat Wolves to move out of the relegation zone in the Premier League.

item.title: Augsburg miss late penalty in tense Hoffenheim draw
item.description: Augsburg's Alexis Claude-Maurice misses a late penalty in an eventful draw with Champions League hopefuls Hoffenheim.

item.title: This season my toughest moment - Watkins
item.description: Aston Villa and England striker Ollie Watkins opens up on his struggles to recapture his form from previous seasons.

item.title: 'It will be smiles' - Nuno on moving out of relegation zone
item.description: West Ham boss Nuno Espirito Santo is "delighted" with Konstantinos Mavropanos, as his side thrash Wolves 4-0 to move out of the Premier League relegation zone.

item.title: 'They punished our mistakes' - Edwards on West Ham thrashing
item.description: Wolverhampton Wanderers boss Rob Edwards reacts to a "difficult night" for his side after their 4-0 loss to West Ham.

item.title: What's next in captivating Scottish Premiership title race?
item.description: Hearts host Motherwell, Celtic welcome St Mirren and Rangers travel to Falkirk in the latest instalment of the fascinating Scottish Premiership title race.

item.title: What are European stakes for Scottish clubs this time?
item.description: Scotland cling on to five European places for one more season, so who gets what at the end of this campaign?

item.title: Liverpool to sign Germany forward Endemann in July
item.description: Liverpool have reached an agreement to sign Germany international Vivien Endemann upon the expiry of her contract at Wolfsburg.

item.title: Spalletti signs new Juventus contract amid Italy links
item.description: Juventus manager Luciano Spalletti signs a two-year contract extension with the club, effectively ruling him out of a return to the Italy national team.

item.title: Wales' Ingle back to best after injury woe
item.description: Midfielder Sophie Ingle bids to continue her impressive return season during Wales' World Cup qualifying double header against Albania.

item.title: Littler v Rooney in nine-dart challenge
item.description: Darts world number one Luke Littler takes on Wayne Rooney in a nine-dart challenge.

item.title: Watch Connolly goal as Queen's Park add to Ross County misery
item.description: Watch Aidan Connolly's first-half headed goal that was enough to give Queen's Park a 1-0 win over Ross County to move up to fifth in the Scottish Championship and leave the visitors two points adrift at the foot of the table.

item.title: The 'unique style' De Zerbi hopes will save Spurs
item.description: BBC Sport's tactics correspondent Umir Irfan breaks down what Roberto de Zerbi will try to implement at Tottenham.

item.title: 'As pure as you can hit it!' Robertson scores against Wolves
item.description: Liverpool left-back Andrew Robertson scores a superb opening goal for his side in their FA Cup fifth round tie against Wolves at Molineux.

item.title: Wilson delighted with Fulham's 'amazing' progress
item.description: Fulham forward Harry Wilson talks about his impressive individual performances, his relationship with manager Marco Silva, and Fulham's progress.

item.title: Ramsey memories are made of this
item.description: BBC Sport Wales takes a look back at some of Aaron Ramsey's career highlights as he announces his retirement.

item.title: It makes English football unique - why the Football League is important too
item.description: Tony Pulis explains why the English Football League is so important and why he loved life in the lower divisions, as well as the lessons he learned.

item.title: Naderi injury serious, says Rangers boss Rohl
item.description: Ryan Naderi's muscle injury is "serious", says Rangers head coach Danny Rohl, who has had more positive news about Tuur Rommens.

item.title: Egypt goalkeeper banned for striking referee
item.description: Al-Ahly goalkeeper Mohamed El-Shenawy banned for four games for striking a referee on the head following an Egyptian Pro League match.

item.title: How Lens embraced underdog status to become PSG's title rivals
item.description: Lens have pushed PSG closer than anyone in Ligue 1 this season and, with the sides still to meet again, still hold an outside shot at the title.

item.title: 'Hungry' Watkins is man on mission - but will Tuchel take notice?
item.description: Ollie Watkins' response to being dropped by England has been nothing short of emphatic, with two strikes in Bologna adding to his goal against West Ham before the international break.

item.title: Premier League must protect teams in Europe - Pereira
item.description: Nottingham Forest boss Vitor Pereira has urged the Premier League to protect teams playing in Europe by easing their schedules.

item.title: Did quadruple ambition set bar too high for Arsenal?
item.description: Monday Night Club's Rory Smith explains why the quadruple was too much for Arsenal.

item.title: Struijk sends Leeds to first FA Cup semi-final since 1987
item.description: Watch Pascal Struijk score the winning penalty in Leeds United's shootout win against West Ham to send the West Yorkshire side to their first FA Cup semi-final in 39 years.

item.title: Lijnders on replacing 'unique' Silva
item.description: Manchester City assistant manager Pep Lijnders discusses captain Bernardo Silva's future at the club, saying "every good story comes to an end".

item.title: Disasi draws West Ham level with 96th-minute equaliser
item.description: West Ham's Axel Disasi athletically prods an equaliser six minutes into stoppage time against Leeds United, forcing extra time in the FA Cup quarter-final.

item.title: Herrick's dramatic debut and Struijk's winner - full penalty shootout
item.description: Watch all the penalties from West Ham's penalty shootout against Leeds United in their FA Cup quarter-final at London Stadium.

item.title: 'Fancy footwork' - Tanaka puts Leeds ahead against West Ham
item.description: Leeds United's Ao Tanaka starts and finishes a well-worked move to give his side the lead against West Ham in their FA Cup quarter-final.

item.title: 'You need to have arrogance' - Rooney and Littler on being number one
item.description: Wayne Rooney and Luke Littler both know what it is like to be thrust into the limelight at a young age.

item.title: Arsenal's big summer - Arteta contract, £100m for Nwaneri and Lewis-Skelly
item.description: Whatever happens this season, Arsenal are facing some big decisions in the summer over Mikel Arteta's contract and their transfer moves.

item.title: Robinson urges togetherness in Aberdeen survival bid
item.description: Stephen Robinson calls for "togetherness" in Aberdeen's relegation battle, saying the time to "scrutinise" is at the end of the season.

item.title: Maguire on his major lows and belief he is still one of world's best
item.description: Manchester United defender Harry Maguire on how his mum defended him in his lowest moments, his return to form, new contract and bright future

item.title: KSI targets Premier League for Dagenham - but not as manager
item.description: YouTuber KSI says he intends to use his social media influence to grow the profile of Dagenham & Redbridge and take them to the Premier League.

item.title: How to follow the Premier League on the BBC this weekend
item.description: BBC Sport Ask Me Anything explains how to follow the Premier League on the BBC this week.

item.title: Barcelona complain to Uefa about VAR in Atletico loss
item.description: Barcelona submit a complaint to Uefa over a "grave lack of VAR intervention" during their Champions League defeat by Atletico Madrid on Wednesday.

item.title: Seven games to go - how will De Zerbi try to save Spurs?
item.description: Clubs at risk of relegation tend to hire pragmatic coaches - but Spurs have not done that by appointing Roberto de Zerbi.

item.title: How are the Scottish Premiership post-split fixtures decided?
item.description: The Scottish Premiership split has again been a controversial talking point, so why does it exist and why is it so complicated?

item.title: How WSL aims to bridge academy gap - and why it needs to
item.description: Chelsea manager Sonia Bompastor raised concerns over the gap between academy football and the first team - so is it an issue in the game and how could it be solved?

item.title: Dropping Fernandez - a sign of Rosenior's authority or a big risk?
item.description: Enzo Fernandez has given Chelsea boss Liam Rosenior the first major internal disciplinary issue of his brief tenure at Stamford Bridge - BBC Sport analyses the situation.

item.title: Back Haaland and Semenyo to shine at Chelsea - FPL team of the week
item.description: Put your faith in trusted FPL assets such as Bruno Fernandes and Antoine Semenyo this week, with a double gameweek on the horizon.

item.title: 54 years, 48 leagues, 2,000 grounds - the ultimate football odyssey
item.description: BBC Sport travels with the UK's ultimate groundhopper to the final match of a decades-long, history-making journey.

item.title: All February and March's done deals, including deadline day
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: How to follow the FA Cup quarter-finals on the BBC
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explain how you can watch, listen and follow the FA Cup quarter-finals.

item.title: How does Scottish Premiership split work?
item.description: The Scottish Premiership title race has been a hot topic all season and it's about to become even more interesting with the league's split.

item.title: How does Tudor's record compare to other Tottenham bosses?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team compare Igor Tudor's managerial record to other Tottenham managers of recent years.

item.title: Have all home nations ever played in same World Cup?
item.description: With England and Scotland already qualified for the 2026 World Cup, Wales and Northern Ireland both face European qualifiers in March.

item.title: Who is one card away from suspension in the Premier League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at who is currently nearing a suspension in the Premier League.

item.title: All you need to know about 2027 Women's World Cup
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team take a look at everything you need to know for the 2027 Women's World Cup.

item.title: When can Arsenal win the Premier League?
item.description: Arsenal are nine points clear at the top of the Premier League, but when can they officially win the title?

item.title: English clubs eye move for Celtic's Nygren - gossip
item.description: Celtic to cash in on Sweden international as the Glasgow club scout midfielder on loan in English Championship.

item.title: Napoli warn they could punish Lukaku over training no-show
item.description: Striker Romelu Lukaku could face disciplinary action after Napoli released a statement to say he has ignored a request to return to the club.

item.title: How former Liverpool prospect found love for football again
item.description: After 18 years at Liverpool, Tyler Morton tells BBC Sport how moving to Lyon in the summer has helped him fall back in love with football.

item.title: From shame to pride - inside Europe's most exciting league
item.description: Poland's top flight has become Europe's most exciting league. Guillem Balague looks at how they have achieved it.

item.title: Griezmann to join MLS side Orlando from Atletico
item.description: Former France forward Antoine Griezmann will join MLS side Orlando City from Atletico Madrid this summer.

item.title: PSG's league game between legs of Liverpool Champions League tie postponed
item.description: Paris St-Germain's Ligue One match against Lens, which was set to take place between the two legs of their Champions League quarter-final against Liverpool, has been postponed.

item.title: How Arbeloa is getting the best out of Real Madrid's stars
item.description: BBC Sport looks at how Alvaro Arbeloa has managed to get the best out of the Real Madrid squad since his appointment in January.

item.title: Real talk is nonsense but I may coach again - Klopp
item.description: Former Liverpool manager Jurgen Klopp says speculation he will return to management with Real Madrid this summer is "nonsense".

item.title: Bedford on 'full circle' moment in England career
item.description: Returning as head coach for England Women's under-23s felt like a "full circle moment" for Lydia Bedford.

item.title: Olid to leave SWPL leaders Hearts at end of season
item.description: Head coach Eva Olid will leave SWPL leaders Hearts at the end of the season by mutual agreement.

item.title: Bay Collective become majority owners of Sunderland Women
item.description: Multi-club ownership organisation Bay Collective has reached an agreement to acquire a majority stake in Sunderland Women.

item.title: When football turned to penalties to end 'cruel' system
item.description: No-one knew what to expect at football's first official penalty shootout - and no-one wanted to go down in history as the first to miss.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.

item.title: The great number nine decline - where have England's strikers gone?
item.description: BBC Sport takes an in-depth look at why England lack centre-forward options behind Harry Kane - a far cry from when the likes of Les Ferdinand only won 17 caps.

item.title: Captain, player, manager, Mister, number one - Davids at Barnet
item.description: BBC Sport remembers Edgar Davids' 15 months as Barnet player-manager in League Two and the Conference.

item.title: From farming village to Liverpool icon - the Salah story
item.description: BBC Sport visits Egypt to discover what Mohamed Salah means to the people of the football-mad country, and how a small boy from humble beginnings became a national icon.

item.title: Napoli 'smell blood' & target Premier League's discarded stars
item.description: After Scott McTominay was a hit, Napoli went back to the Premier League for Kevin de Bruyne, could more players follow them to Serie A?
